title: Catarine Towani
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:CatarineTowani.png]] |name=Catarine Towani |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} establishes that ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' and ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' take place three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Catarine Towani's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] must also therefore take place in 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Towani family]] |parents= |partners=[[Jeremitt Towani]] |siblings= |children=*[[Cindel Towani]] *[[Mace Towani]] |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Please, Mace. We're not mad at you!|Catarine desperately calling for her children|}} '''Catarine Towani''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who had two [[Youngling/Legends|children]] with her [[Marriage/Legends|husband]], [[Jeremitt Towani|Jeremitt]]: [[Cindel Towani|Cindel]] and [[Mace Towani|Mace]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Towani family]] was marooned on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] after their [[Towani family star cruiser|star cruiser]] crashed there. While stranded, Catarine and Jeremitt temporarily left their children by the [[Starship/Legends|starship]], instructing them to stay there, but when they returned, Cindel and Mace were gone. Catarine and Jeremitt tried to search for them, but as they did so, they were captured by a [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]], who took them to his [[Forbidden Fortress of the Gorax|lair]]. Cindel and Mace later teamed up with the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] to find their [[Parent/Legends|parents]] and go to the Gorax's lair in order to bring them home. The children-and-Ewok party arrived, reuniting with Catarine and Jeremitt by distracting the Gorax. Following a short skirmish with the beast, in which they suffered a single Ewok [[Chukha-Trok/Legends|loss]], they were able to rescue the captives and return to the Ewoks' [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Later that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the village was [[Raid on Bright Tree Village|invaded]] by [[Sanyassan Marauders]], and Catarine, Mace, and Jeremitt were among the [[Death/Legends|casualties]]. ==Biography== ===Marooned on Endor=== {{Quote|I knew we shouldn't have left them alone, not even for a minute.|Catarine Towani|}} [[File:SearchForKids-COC.png|thumb|left|300px|The Towani parents search for their children.]] Catarine Towani''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to the Human [[Jeremitt Towani]]. The pair had two [[Youngling/Legends|children]] [[Towani family|together]]: [[Mace Towani|Mace]],{{DB|character|macetowani|Towani, Mace}} who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[11 BBY/Legends|11 BBY]],{{DB|character|macetowani|Towani, Mace}} establishes that [[Mace Towani]] was fourteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old during the events of ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'', which "[[Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline]]" establishes occurs three years after {{Film|IV}}. As that corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', he must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[11 BBY/Legends|11 BBY]]. and [[Cindel Towani|Cindel]], born nine [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[2 BBY/Legends|later]].{{DB|character|cindeltowani|Towani, Cindel}} In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Towanis were traveling home in their [[Towani family star cruiser|family star cruiser]], with Catarine and Jeremitt [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]], but they crashed on''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Due to their being marooned in the [[Forest/Legends|forest]], Catarine and Jeremitt asked their children to stay in the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] while they made a vain attempt to locate civilization, but Mace, who was prone to ignoring instructions, left the ship with his sister. Catarine and Jeremitt later returned, terrified to find their children missing. They began searching for them with [[Glowrod/Legends|flashlights]], calling out their names and splitting up. The two regrouped back at the [[Star Cruiser/Legends|star cruiser]], where Catarine asked her husband if he had found Cindel and Mace, which he had not. She reminded Jeremitt that each [[Standard day/Legends|day]] since they had crashed, they had both given reminders to the children to stay by the ship. She then lamented their decision to leave them alone in order to find a [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] since they were only young children who would not know what to do. Jeremitt rechecked the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], with Catarine calling out their names once again. She also shouted that she and their [[Parent/Legends|father]] were not mad at them for leaving. It was then that Jeremitt found Cindel's [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]] on the ground. Catarine noted aloud that she hoped her daughter had kept on her [[Life monitor/Legends|life monitor]]. As they continued searching, Jeremitt embraced his wife, assuring her that their children would be found. However, their cries for Mace and Cindel attracted the attention of a [[Gorax/Legends|Gorax]] beast who, after chasing after them with a large [[Axe/Legends|axe]], captured the Humans and took them to his [[Forbidden Fortress of the Gorax|fortress]]. Mace and Cindel were later located by a [[Bright Tree Village tribe|tribe]] of [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], who used [[Magic/Legends|magic]] to find where Catarine and Jeremitt had been taken as the children longed to be reunited with their parents. They helped them assemble a caravan to find the Gorax's lair, despite no Ewok having ever returned from the [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] prior. ===Saved by the caravan=== {{Quote|Hurry!|Catarine, to Mace|}} [[File:TowanisRescued.png|thumb|right|300px|Catarine and Jeremitt's rescue]] The Ewoks arrived at the lair and made their way to the Gorax, where they found the parents locked up in a cage that hung from the ceiling. Jeremitt held Catarine in his [[Limb/Legends|arms]] as the Gorax came close to them, but one of the Ewoks was able to cause a distraction that made the Gorax chase after him and the others. Once the Gorax was—albeit temporarily—out of sight, Mace and the remaining Ewoks devised a plan to get the Humans down from the cage, which involved using an [[Melee weapon/Legends|axe]] to [[Catapult/Legends|catapult]] Mace upward. Jeremitt then noticed that Mace had arrived, which he excitedly told Catarine. Once Mace was launched upward, he reunited with his parents and let down a long [[Rope/Legends|rope]], up which the Ewok [[Chukha-Trok/Legends|Chukha-Trok]] then climbed. Jeremitt was confused by the Ewok, but his son assured him that he was a friend. With Chukha-Trok and Jeremitt holding the rope, Mace went back down, intending to bring his parents next, with his mother urging him to hurry. However, the Gorax then returned, but his attention was turned to a runaway [[Weechee Warrick|Ewok]], so Jeremitt and Catarine were able to safely descend. It took a bit longer for Chukha-Trok, whose rope broke, causing him to fall to the ground. With everyone down, the party rushed out, but Chukha-Trok stayed behind to fight, stabbing the Gorax's foot. The party was stopped when they reached a cliff, with Catarine noticing that Mace was missing. The two parents had a realization, both worrying for their son. They rushed back, calling his name, to find him trying to fight the Gorax by using [[Izrina]] the [[Wistie/Legends|Wistie]]. She successfully aggravated the Gorax, driving him away, but in his anger, he triggered a rockfall onto Chukha-Trok, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. Mace said goodbye and thanked him, but the battle was not won yet, for the Gorax had returned. Jeremitt and an Ewok assembled a rope to trip up the Gorax, which worked, but he did not fully fall into the gorge. The Ewok [[Kaink/Legends|Kaink]] tried to use her magic to push him over the edge, but was unsuccessful. As the Gorax tried to get up again, Catarine shot his shoulder with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], giving him the necessary push over the edge. ===Final escape and celebrating a victory=== {{Quote|We thought you were dead.''"
"''Oh, darling!|Cindel and Catarine|}} [[File:TowanisReunited-CoC.png|thumb|left|300px|The Towani family bar Cindel reunites.]] Once the Gorax was gone, Catarine gave Jeremitt a hug. Mace then ran over to his parents, hugging them as well, with Catarine telling him that she had thought he was lost forever. Cindel and her Ewok friend [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] then arrived; Cindel waved to her mother, with both of them excited to see each other. However, the Gorax returned once again, having climbed out of the gorge. The party of Ewoks and Humans screamed in fear, but Mace threw Chukha-Trok's axe at his neck, sending him over the cliff once more, this [[Time/Legends|time]] for good. Mace used the rope to swing to the other side of the ravine, with the Ewoks and his parents following suit. There, Catarine reunited with Cindel, who confessed that she thought her parents had been killed. Izrina then returned as well, leading the caravan back to the Ewok home of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. A party was held to celebrate the return of Catarine and Jeremitt, where there was Ewok [[Music/Legends|music]] and dancing. Wicket's [[Warrick family/Legends|mother]], [[Shodu Warrick|Shodu]], handed an item to Catarine, and Wicket also gifted her a [[Flower/Legends|flower]]. She thanked both of them for the gifts. ===Death and legacy=== {{Quote|My family, they're all dead. What happens to me now, Wicket? I'm all alone.|Cindel Towani|''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''}} [[File:Catarine-EtBfE.png|thumb|right|300px|Mace taking his mother into a hut]] Later that year, the Ewok village was [[Raid on Bright Tree Village|attacked]] by a group of [[Sanyassan Marauders]]. Wicket and Cindel were away for the initial siege, so managed to survive, but Catarine was present, and her health quickly deteriorated so that Cindel's life monitor alerted her to her mother's dire situation. The young Human ran over to her, finding Mace fighting off the Sanyassans next to their mother's lifeless body. Mace shouted at his sister to get away and find their father, dragging Catarine into a nearby hut and continuing to fire his blaster at the Marauders. However, Jeremitt was also under attack; he briefly escaped, but Catarine and Mace did not survive. Jeremitt consoled Cindel for their deaths, instructing his young daughter to save herself, leave him, and join the Ewoks. He soon died as well. A few days later, a [[Witch/Legends|witch]] named [[Charal]] successfully lured Cindel into a trap by [[Music/Legends|singing]] her a song that her mother used to sing to her.{{FactFile|123|TOW 1-4|Cindel Towani}} A year later, in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' junior novelization]] depicts the events of {{Film|VI}}, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. another Human female, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], traveled to Endor and encountered Wicket. The Ewok was reminded of Catarine when he saw Organa.[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' junior novelization]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|We thought we'd lost you.|Catarine, to Mace|}} Catarine Towani was in anguish at the prospect of her children being lost, and regretted the decision to leave them alone after they crashed on Endor. She was relieved when Mace rescued her and Jeremitt from the Gorax, which confirmed Mace and Cindel were alive, but she was still nervous to get out, biting her nails in apprehension. She cautioned her son as he climbed down the rope, worried for him. She had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin. ==Equipment== Towani wore white [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] with a white jacket. She utilized a blaster to shoot the Gorax. ==Behind the scenes== Catarine Towani first appeared in the [[1984]] television film ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'', where she was portrayed by [[Fionnula Flanagan]]. ''Caravan of Courage'' was filmed in [[Wikipedia:Marin County|Marin County]], [[Wikipedia:California|California]] between [[June]] and [[August]] of that year.{{SW|url=news/caravan-of-courage-celebrating-30-years-of-an-ewok-adventure|text=''Caravan of Courage'': Celebrating 30 Years of an Ewok Adventure}} The portrayer of Catarine's corpse in the [[1985]] film ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' was not credited. Her last name was confirmed to be "Towani," akin to her family name, in the [[1998]] reference book ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. In the [[2022]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga]]'', one of the side missions on Endor is to fix a painting of the Towani family, including Catarine, that has been broken into pieces, though their name is not mentioned in the game.''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga]]'' ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} *''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'' *[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' junior novelization]] {{Imo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Catarine Towani}} *''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{InsiderCite|32|Caravan of Courage: Star Wars on Television|''Caravan of Courage'': ''Star Wars'' on Television}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{1stID|Towani}} *{{FactFile|123|TOW 1-4|Cindel Towani}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Special Edition]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|110|Warriors of Endor|reprint=1}} *{{DB|species|gorax|Gorax}} *{{DB|character|cindeltowani|Towani, Cindel}} *{{DB|character|macetowani|Towani, Mace}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' *{{InsiderCite|229|Era of the Ewoks}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Catarine Towani |nl=Catarine Towani }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Towani, Catarine}} [[Category:Endorians]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]]
